mathematical_definition: Q_i = max(|dH/dt|/(sigma_Hdot+eps), |d2M/dt2|/(sigma_Mddot+eps)); sigma_Hdot = 1.4826*MAD(dH/dt on tail), sigma_Mddot = 1.4826*MAD(d2M/dt2 on tail)
operational_rule: Use tail = last 20% of points in each trace to estimate robust noise scales before onset detection.
physical_rationale: Separates field-switch dynamics (high dH/dt) and instrument/magnetic settling (high d2M/dt2) from true relaxation.
robustness_rationale: Noise-normalized score avoids fixed absolute thresholds and scales across temperatures and amplitudes.

mathematical_definition: t0 = min t_i such that median(Q_i..Q_{i+w-1}) <= 3 and sign(dM/dt) is constant on i..i+w-1, with w = max(5, ceil(0.05*N))
operational_rule: t0 is the first sustained entry into low-transient, single-direction relaxation regime.
physical_rationale: Start of relaxation is defined by disappearance of field-switch and curvature transient, not by file index.
robustness_rationale: Uses robust statistics plus persistence; insensitive to one-point glitches and small preprocessing changes.

mathematical_definition: tau_i = t_i - t0; tau_min = median(diff(t_j)) for j in first w post-t0 points; t_start = t0 + tau_min
operational_rule: Window starts at first resolvable post-t0 log-time sample (tau > 0).
physical_rationale: Avoids log singularity at tau=0 while keeping onset anchored to physical t0.
robustness_rationale: Depends on local sampling cadence, not arbitrary absolute delay.

mathematical_definition: Compute local R_i = -dM/dln(tau) on moving window w; sigma_R_tail = 1.4826*MAD(R_i on last 20% post-t0 points); t_end = max t_i with |median(R_{i-w+1..i})| >= 3*sigma_R_tail and sign(R_{i-w+1..i}) = sign(median(R_post_t0))
operational_rule: Stop when relaxation signal reaches noise floor or loses relaxation sign coherence.
physical_rationale: Excludes late-time noise-dominated tail while preserving physically meaningful decay.
robustness_rationale: SNR-based and sign-coherence based; reproducible across temperatures without fixed end indices.

mathematical_definition: R_relax_canonical(T) = -b1 where (b0,b1) minimize sum rho(M_i - (b0 + b1*ln(tau_i))) for tau_i in [tau_min, t_end-t0], with Huber loss rho
operational_rule: Report only this one scalar per temperature.
physical_rationale: Directly measures mean magnetic viscosity (average -dM/dln(tau)) in the physical relaxation regime.
robustness_rationale: Robust fit downweights outliers and avoids model-family ambiguity (no mixed tau vs S definitions).
